global.title: Trump says expecting Iranian response to latest US proposal ‘tonight’ - The Times of Israel
global.summary: The U.S. says it expects Iran’s reply to a new proposal to end the war soon, while also sanctioning people and firms tied to Iran’s drone production. The moment suggests diplomacy is still open, but fragile.
global.whyItMatters: This is a key prayer point for peace, restraint, and protection of civilians as conflict and diplomacy move side by side.

global.title: A federal court strikes down Trump’s 10% across-the-board tariffs - CNN
global.summary: A U.S. federal court ruled against the administration’s attempt to impose broad tariffs, adding fresh uncertainty to global trade and economic planning. The decision comes as businesses and households already face instability.
global.whyItMatters: Economic uncertainty affects jobs, giving, and family stress—important context for pastoral care and preaching about trust and provision.

global.title: Deadly volcanic eruption in Indonesia leaves hikers missing - CNN
global.summary: A rescue effort is underway after Mount Dukono erupted, with more than a dozen hikers reportedly missing. The disaster adds to a week of global instability and human suffering.
global.whyItMatters: Natural disasters create opportunities for prayer, compassion, and reminding congregations of the church’s global concern for the vulnerable.

canadian.title: Canada Unemployment Rate Rises to Six-Month High in April - WSJ
canadian.summary: Statistics Canada data show the unemployment rate rose to 6.9% in April as employers cut a net 17,700 jobs. More Canadians are looking for work while the labour market continues to soften.
canadian.whyItMatters: Job loss and financial anxiety often surface first in pastoral conversations, benevolence needs, and family strain.

canadian.title: TSX edge higher on ceasefire optimism; strong US jobs data - KITCO
canadian.summary: Markets rose modestly, but the report highlights a weaker Canadian labour picture than the U.S., with tariffs and trade uncertainty still weighing on Canada. Investor optimism has not erased underlying economic fragility.
canadian.whyItMatters: This helps frame the national mood: some market calm, but real pressure on workers and households remains.

canadian.title: Officials Racing To Contract Trace Potentially Infected Hantavirus Cruise Passengers—Including Americans (Live Updates) - Forbes
canadian.summary: Health officials are tracing passengers from a cruise ship linked to a hantavirus outbreak, with Canada among the countries involved. The story reflects ongoing public-health vigilance around cross-border travel.
canadian.whyItMatters: Pastors may want awareness for travelers, vulnerable seniors, and anyone already carrying health-related fear.

persecutedChurch.title: Israeli suspected of attacking French nun in Jerusalem charged with religious hate crime - Reuters
persecutedChurch.summary: Authorities have charged a suspect in the assault of a French nun in Jerusalem, treating the case as a religious hate crime. The charge is a concrete legal development after wider concern over hostility toward Christians.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This gives a specific update for prayer and advocacy: justice is being pursued, even as broader fears for Christian safety remain.

persecutedChurch.title: For Christians in Israel and Jerusalem, intolerance is becoming normal - Al Jazeera
persecutedChurch.summary: Christians in Israel and East Jerusalem say harassment, assault, and intimidation have become increasingly routine, with many incidents going unreported. Community leaders describe a climate of fear and diminishing confidence in protection.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: It is a sobering reminder to pray for the global body of Christ and to teach congregations about solidarity with suffering believers.

leadership.title: Why workforce wellbeing can no longer sit on the sidelines in ECEC - The Sector
leadership.summary: This piece argues that wellbeing should be built into leadership, systems, and organizational design rather than treated as an afterthought. It connects healthy workplaces with safety, retention, and sustainable service.
leadership.whyItMatters: A pastor leading staff and volunteers can take this as a reminder that culture, safety, and endurance are shaped by systems—not just good intentions.

goodNews.title: Cubs and White Sox fans come together to celebrate kidney donation - NBC News
goodNews.summary: A Chicago Cubs fan in need of a kidney received one from a White Sox fan, and the two were later honored together at a Cubs game. It is a simple, joyful story of generosity crossing rivalry lines.
goodNews.whyItMatters: This is a vivid illustration of neighbor-love, sacrificial care, and the kind of human goodness that preachers can point to with gratitude.
